source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the figure, points $D$ and $E$ are the midpoints of sides $BC$ and $AB$ of the equilateral triangle $\triangle ABC$, respectively, with $AD=6$. If $F$ is a moving point on $AD$, what is the minimum value of $BF+EF$?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text:
\textbf{Solution:} Connect CE, intersecting AD at F, and connect BF, then BF+EF is the minimum (according to the fact that the shortest distance between two points is a straight line; the perpendicular distance from a point to a line is the shortest),\\
$\because$E is the midpoint of AB, $\triangle ABC$ is an equilateral triangle,\\
$\therefore CE\perp AB$\\
Since C and B are symmetric about AD, then BF+EF=CF,\\
$\because$In the equilateral $\triangle ABC$, BD=CD,\\
$\therefore$AD$\perp$BC,\\
$\therefore$AD is the perpendicular bisector of BC (three lines in one),\\
$\therefore$C and B are symmetric about the line AD,\\
$\therefore$CF=BF,\\
That is, BF+EF=CF+EF=CE,\\
$\because$AD$\perp$BC, CE$\perp$AB,\\
$\therefore$$\angle$ADB=$\angle$CEB=$90^\circ$,\\
In $\triangle ADB$ and $\triangle CEB$,\\
$\left\{\begin{array}{l}
\angle ADB=\angle CEB\\
\angle ABD=\angle CBE\\
AB=CB
\end{array}\right.$ ,\\
$\therefore$$\triangle ADB\cong \triangle CEB$ (AAS),\\
$\therefore$CE=AD=6,\\
That is, BF+EF=\boxed{6}.

题目同时结合图形关系与最值目标，具备较明显的多步多模态推理潜力。

normalization_record.normalized_question_text: As shown in the figure, points $D$ and $E$ are the midpoints of sides $BC$ and $AB$ of the equilateral triangle $\triangle ABC$, respectively, with $AD=6$. If $F$ is a moving point on $AD$, what is the minimum value of $BF+EF$?
normalization_record.normalized_answer_text: Connect $CE$, intersecting $AD$ at $F$, and connect $BF$. Then $BF+EF$ is minimized. Since $E$ is the midpoint of $AB$ and $\triangle ABC$ is equilateral, $CE\perp AB$. Also, $D$ is the midpoint of $BC$, so $AD\perp BC$ and $AD$ is the perpendicular bisector of $BC$; hence $B$ and $C$ are symmetric about $AD$, so $BF=CF$. Therefore, $BF+EF=CF+EF=CE$. In $\triangle ADB$ and $\triangle CEB$, $\angle ADB=\angle CEB=90^\circ$, $\angle ABD=\angle CBE$, and $AB=CB$, so $\triangle ADB\cong\triangle CEB$. Thus $CE=AD=6$, so the minimum value of $BF+EF$ is $6$.

normalized_value.expected_answer: Connect $CE$, intersecting $AD$ at $F$, and connect $BF$. Then $BF+EF$ is minimized. Since $E$ is the midpoint of $AB$ and $\triangle ABC$ is equilateral, $CE\perp AB$. Also, $D$ is the midpoint of $BC$, so $AD\perp BC$ and $AD$ is the perpendicular bisector of $BC$; hence $B$ and $C$ are symmetric about $AD$, so $BF=CF$. Therefore, $BF+EF=CF+EF=CE$. In $\triangle ADB$ and $\triangle CEB$, $\angle ADB=\angle CEB=90^\circ$, $\angle ABD=\angle CBE$, and $AB=CB$, so $\triangle ADB\cong\triangle CEB$. Thus $CE=AD=6$, so the minimum value of $BF+EF$ is $6$.

field_audit_records.before_value:
\textbf{Solution:} Connect CE, intersecting AD at F, and connect BF, then BF+EF is the minimum (according to the fact that the shortest distance between two points is a straight line; the perpendicular distance from a point to a line is the shortest),\\
$\because$E is the midpoint of AB, $\triangle ABC$ is an equilateral triangle,\\
$\therefore CE\perp AB$\\
Since C and B are symmetric about AD, then BF+EF=CF,\\
$\because$In the equilateral $\triangle ABC$, BD=CD,\\
$\therefore$AD$\perp$BC,\\
$\therefore$AD is the perpendicular bisector of BC (three lines in one),\\
$\therefore$C and B are symmetric about the line AD,\\
$\therefore$CF=BF,\\
That is, BF+EF=CF+EF=CE,\\
$\because$AD$\perp$BC, CE$\perp$AB,\\
$\therefore$$\angle$ADB=$\angle$CEB=$90^\circ$,\\
In $\triangle ADB$ and $\triangle CEB$,\\
$\left\{\begin{array}{l}
\angle ADB=\angle CEB\\
\angle ABD=\angle CBE\\
AB=CB
\end{array}\right.$ ,\\
$\therefore$$\triangle ADB\cong \triangle CEB$ (AAS),\\
$\therefore$CE=AD=6,\\
That is, BF+EF=\boxed{6}.
field_audit_records.after_value: Connect $CE$, intersecting $AD$ at $F$, and connect $BF$. Then $BF+EF$ is minimized. Since $E$ is the midpoint of $AB$ and $\triangle ABC$ is equilateral, $CE\perp AB$. Also, $D$ is the midpoint of $BC$, so $AD\perp BC$ and $AD$ is the perpendicular bisector of $BC$; hence $B$ and $C$ are symmetric about $AD$, so $BF=CF$. Therefore, $BF+EF=CF+EF=CE$. In $\triangle ADB$ and $\triangle CEB$, $\angle ADB=\angle CEB=90^\circ$, $\angle ABD=\angle CBE$, and $AB=CB$, so $\triangle ADB\cong\triangle CEB$. Thus $CE=AD=6$, so the minimum value of $BF+EF$ is $6$.

variants.title: MM-Math 开放题
variants.rewritten_question_text: As shown in the figure, points $D$ and $E$ are the midpoints of sides $BC$ and $AB$ of the equilateral triangle $\triangle ABC$, respectively, with $AD=6$. If $F$ is a moving point on $AD$, what is the minimum value of $BF+EF$?
variants.expected_answer: Connect $CE$, intersecting $AD$ at $F$, and connect $BF$. Then $BF+EF$ is minimized. Since $E$ is the midpoint of $AB$ and $\triangle ABC$ is equilateral, $CE\perp AB$. Also, $D$ is the midpoint of $BC$, so $AD\perp BC$ and $AD$ is the perpendicular bisector of $BC$; hence $B$ and $C$ are symmetric about $AD$, so $BF=CF$. Therefore, $BF+EF=CF+EF=CE$. In $\triangle ADB$ and $\triangle CEB$, $\angle ADB=\angle CEB=90^\circ$, $\angle ABD=\angle CBE$, and $AB=CB$, so $\triangle ADB\cong\triangle CEB$. Thus $CE=AD=6$, so the minimum value of $BF+EF$ is $6$.
